factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US Deal)
factors.summary: Major headline: Iran-US peace deal framework near completion with Hormuz set to reopen; multiple sources confirm signing in coming days. Risk-off de-escalation typically weighs on safe-haven demand for gold, though initial market reaction showed modest upside as inflation anxiety persists.

factors.name: Oil Price (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: US crude fell below $85 on Friday as Iran deal progress signaled Hormuz reopening; lower oil reduces near-term inflation input and removes geopolitical premium. This eases Fed pressure, which is initially gold-negative but longer-term bullish via lower real yields.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY lost ground during the week (down 0.27% to ~99.80) ahead of heavy central bank calendar; risk-on sentiment on Iran deal framework typically weakens the dollar. Weaker USD is a direct structural bullish factor for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data in news window, but lower nominal yields expected if geopolitical premium unwinds and Fed holds or cuts. Without fresh CPI/inflation data, real yields remain the dominant unknown—critical to monitor for next 24-72h.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: No FOMC decision or Fed speak in latest news cycle. Upcoming central bank calendar (Fed, BoJ, BoE, RBA decisions) noted for next week; no hawkish surprise yet. This is a key upcoming catalyst that could shift bias sharply if Fed signals higher-for-longer rates.

factors.name: Inflation Data (CPI/PCE/PPI)
factors.summary: No recent inflation prints in news window. Social Security COLA for 2027 may jump to 4.7% per one estimate, signaling persistent inflation concerns, but this is secondary to actual CPI/PCE releases due this week or next.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Breakevens
factors.summary: Market participants remain optimistic about middle-east stabilization, but underlying inflation anxiety persists (noted in analyst commentary on gold clearing $4,200). 5y5y breakevens likely stable; no deflationary shock evident.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment (VIX, Equities)
factors.summary: Major indices closed higher on the day and week; SpaceX IPO success ($75B, +19% day-one) and Iran deal optimism fueled risk-on. However, gold also cleared $4,200 on Friday, suggesting dual safe-haven + real-yield support despite equity strength.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Central Bank Buying
factors.summary: No fresh data on GLD/IAU holdings or PBoC/RBI buying in this news cycle. Structural CB demand remains in place but not a near-term driver this week.

factors.name: Physical Demand Seasonality
factors.summary: No mention of Indian wedding season or Chinese holiday demand; out of primary seasonal bid windows for now.

screening.narrative: Gold has cleared and consolidated above $4,200 on Friday as market participants reconciled two opposing forces: (1) risk-off geopolitical de-escalation (Iran-US deal near signing, Hormuz reopening, oil down) which usually weighs on safe-haven demand, versus (2) persistent inflation anxiety and weakening USD as markets anticipate lower real yields if the Fed responds to falling oil/geopolitical premium with dovish bias. The 24h +0.66% gain and flatness in 4h (-0.04%) suggests the move is consolidating after a Phase 2 rally rather than extending. Upcoming central bank decisions (Fed, BoJ, BoE, RBA) and fresh inflation data (CPI/PCE likely this week) are critical wildcards that could flip the bias sharply.
catalysts_ahead: Fed FOMC decision & dot plot (likely mid-late June per calendar)
US CPI/Core CPI release (scheduled early-mid June)
PCE/Core PCE (may be released this week or next)
BoJ, BoE, RBA policy decisions (week of June 16-20)
Iran deal signing (next few days per Trump admin)
Oil market reaction to actual Hormuz supply flow changes
screening.risk_warning: If the Iran deal is formally signed AND holds, oil stabilizes below $85, and the Fed signals hawkish hold or rate hikes (not cuts) in response to sticky inflation data, gold could reverse sharply lower as real yields rise and USD strengthens. Conversely, if central banks pivot dovish or CPI surprises to the downside, gold could break higher past $4,250. The move is already +0.66% in 24h and consolidating, so late entries are higher-risk; wait for a pullback toward $4,215-$4,220 for better risk/reward into the central bank decision gauntlet.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 consolidation; if bullish bias holds, look for pullback retest of $4,215-$4,220 before considering fresh longs. Do not chase above $4,250 without confirmation of Fed dovishness or fresh CPI miss. Shorts risky until real yields data clarifies Fed stance post-Iran-deal.
